title: Overcoming poverty through dryland agriculture a strategy for India.Policy Brief No. 6
abstract:
The ‘New Deal’ to rural India is aligned with a ‘pro-poor
people centred ‘ perspective for development. It underlines
growth based on efficiency and equity and stimulates the
agricultural economy to boost incomes, demand and growth
across the vast rural heartland, home to 72% of India’s
population.
publication: Strategic Assessments and Development Pathways for Agriculture in the Semi-Arid Tropics
